title: Varying the natural sound of a drum using a drum attachment
abstract: Particular embodiments described herein provide for a drum that includes a body, a skin, an attachment point, a support arm that can travel back and forth through the attachment point, and an accessory, wherein when the accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin, a natural sound of the drum is varied when the drum is struck. In an example, the accessory is removable and can be replaced with a different accessory that will cause a different sound to be produced when the different accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin and the drum is struck.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A drum comprising: a body; a skin; an attachment point; a support arm that can travel back and forth through the attachment point; and an accessory, wherein when the accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin, a natural sound of the drum is varied when the drum is struck. 2. The drum of Claim 1, wherein the attachment point is located on the body of the drum. S. The drum of any one of Claims 1 and 2, wherein a plurality of attachment points are located on the body of the drum. 4. The drum of any one of Claims 1-3, wherein the attachment point is located on a rim of the drum. 5. The drum of any one of Claims 1-4, wherein the attachment point is removable. 6. The drum of any one of Claims 1-5, wherein the accessory is removable and can be replaced with a different accessory that will cause a different sound to be produced when the different accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin and the drum is struck. 7. A method comprising: positioning an accessory on a support arm of a drum, wherein the drum includes: a body; a skin; and an attachment point, wherein the support arm that can travel back and forth through the attachment point; and 

 using the support arm to position the accessory on or proximate to the skin to vary a natural sound of the drum when the drum is struck. 8. The method of Claim 7, further comprising: using the support arm to position the accessory away from the skin so the drum produces the natural sound of the drum when the drum is struck. 9. The method of any one of Claims 7 and 8, further comprising: removing the accessory from the support arm; adding a new accessory to the support arm; and using the support arm to position the new accessory on or proximate to the skin to vary a natural sound of the drum when the drum is struck. 10. The method of any one of Claims 7-9, wherein the attachment point is located on the body of the drum. 11. The method of any one of Claims 7-10, wherein a plurality of attachment points are located on the body of the drum. 12. The method of any one of Claims 7-11, wherein the attachment point is located on a rim of the drum. 13. The method of any one of Claims 7-12, wherein the drum is a base drum. 14. The method of any one of Claims 7-13, wherein the accessory can be moved towards the skin in less than one second. 15. A system for changing a natural sound of a drum, the system comprising: an accessory; a support arm; and 

 an attachment point, wherein the attachment point is located on a drum that includes a body, a rim, and a skin, wherein the support arm can travel back and forth through the attachment point, wherein the accessory is attached to the support arm and when the accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin, a natural sound of the drum is varied when the drum is struck. 16. The system of Claim 15, wherein the accessory is removable from the support arm. 17. The system of any one of Claims 15 and 16, wherein the accessory is removable from the support arm and can be replaced with a different accessory that will cause a different sound to be produced when the different accessory is positioned on or proximate to the skin and the drum is struck. 18. The system of any one of Claims 15-17, wherein the attachment point is located on the body of the drum. 19. The system of any one of Claims 15-18, wherein a plurality of attachment points are located on the body of the drum. 20. The system of any one of Claims 15-19, wherein the attachment point is located on a rim of the drum.
